tech_news.title: Apple debuts M5 Pro and M5 Max to supercharge the most demanding pro workflows - Apple
tech_news.description: Apple today announced M5 Pro and M5 Max, the world’s most advanced chips for pro laptops, powering the new MacBook Pro.

tech_news.title: Highguard to Permanently Shut Down on March 12, Wildlight to Release One Final Update With a New Warden, a New Weapon, Skill Trees, and More - IGN
tech_news.description: Wildlight has announced that it will be permanently shutting down Highguard on March 12. Before then, it will release one final update that adds a new Warden, a new weapon, account level progression, and skill trees for those who want to get their final match…

tech_news.title: A Possible US Government iPhone-Hacking Toolkit Is Now in the Hands of Foreign Spies and Criminals - WIRED
tech_news.description: A highly sophisticated set of iPhone hijacking techniques has likely infected tens of thousands of phones or more. Clues suggest it was originally built for the US government.

tech_news.title: The Pixel Watch now lets you tap to pay without opening the Wallet app - The Verge
tech_news.description: Google is launching a bunch of updates for the Pixel Watch, including a new “express mode” feature that lets you tap to pay without opening your wallet app.

tech_news.title: New MacBooks, the iPhone 17E, and more: Everything we know about Apple’s March 2026 announcements - The Verge
tech_news.description: Apple is making a bunch of product announcements, including for the iPhone, iPad, and for MacBooks, ahead of a “special Apple experience” on March 4th.

tech_news.title: Amid new competition, Chrome speeds up its release schedule - TechCrunch
tech_news.description: Google Chrome will begin shipping new releases every two weeks in September, instead of monthly.

tech_news.title: Apple May Add AirPlay-Like Feature to CarPlay, Making Tesla Integration More Compelling - Not a Tesla App
tech_news.description: Apple is reportedly bringing native Apple TV support to CarPlay, just as Tesla works on officially adding the infotainment system to its vehicles

tech_news.title: Apple’s website leaks MacBook ‘Neo,’ which could be its new cheaper laptop - The Verge
tech_news.description: A listing on a regulatory page on Apple’s website appeared to leak the “MacBook Neo,” which could be an entry-level MacBook Apple has reportedly been developing.
